reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome BEFORE building — the real ONNX graphs repainted photos in painterly styles, with a rendered-output screenshot reviewed; then the full demo was driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver across all 5 pages; desktop+mobile 0px overflow measured), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21, WASM, fresh profile) BEFORE building. The real fast-neural-style ONNX graphs (onnxmodelzoo/{candy,mosaic,udnie,pointilism,rain-princess}-9) loaded via ort.InferenceSession.create. inputNames=['input1'] (NCHW), outputNames=['output1']; the export is FIXED at [1,3,224,224] (confirmed: a 384² feed was rejected with 'invalid dimensions … Expected 224'). On real photos each style produced a genuine painted restyle: normalized RMSE(out,in) 0.19-0.32, and a RENDERED-OUTPUT SCREENSHOT was reviewed (city street -> Afremov 'Rain Princess' impressionist brushwork). NOT a colour filter (RMSE far above a curve), NOT identity, NOT degenerate (output std ~67 in the 0-255 range). Then the deployed demo ran end-to-end: Paint produced real output at inference 374 ms (restyle amount 0.187).
rubric.notes: Inference AND the 224²->display resize composite run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the styled ImageBitmap is transferred (not cloned) back. The worker handles the un-normalised 0-255 byte range + NCHW layout by hand.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a painted image [1,3,224,224] (NCHW) in ~[0,255], clamped and resized back to the photo's aspect (the network is fixed at 224²). Semantic sanity: the five styles diverge on the SAME input (mosaic tiles vs pointillist dots vs Afremov swirls) and the restyle-amount metric tracks the change — evidence of five distinct learned generators, not one filter with presets.
rubric.notes: A distinct stylization technique vs the AnimeGANv2 cartoonizer built the same wave: this is per-artwork feed-forward perceptual-loss style transfer (Johnson 2016), the model family that started real-time neural style transfer.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model ids (onnxmodelzoo/{candy,mosaic,udnie,pointilism,rain-princess}-9, linked), the task (image-to-image / neural style transfer), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no style-transfer class/task), the real I/O tensor names + shapes AND the FIXED 224² NCHW 0-255 range (in at-a-glance + the How-the-API-works snippet), quantisation (fp32 single file per style), backend (WASM), download (~6.6 MB/style, lazy) and license (Apache-2.0, ONNX Model Zoo). No invented API surface.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (the isolated per-worker ORT-web escape hatch) — shared lib/webai.js is untouched. Styles load lazily so only picked styles download.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnxmodelzoo/candy-9', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:7}). Each style is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (cache name fast-neural-style-onnx-cache) under its own repo path so lib/model-cache.js scans '/onnxmodelzoo/candy-9/' and a returning visit auto-initialises Candy with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~6.6 MB). Verified in headless Chrome: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it downloaded + flipped the loader to ready. A per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: The loader tracks the default style (Candy) as the representative; switching styles lazily downloads ~6.6 MB with an on-page status. Small models keep the cost low.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error states are announced in role=status aria-live regions (shared loader + page #status). The Paint status discloses when a first-use style downloads ~6.6 MB. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Painting failed: …'); the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere (conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds).
rubric.notes: Measured 0 console errors and 0 network failures on load AND run across all 5 pages.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the 5 style chips (role=group, aria-pressed toggle, ≥2.4rem tap height), the accessible before/after reveal range (aria-valuetext '% painted'), the sample gallery (rights-safe thumbnails as labelled buttons), the upload/drop dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space, drag+drop), the Paint button, and a Download-painting anchor. Ladder pages add: wild's five-style gallery wall; multi-model's second stylizer (AnimeGANv2).
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built image-to-image demos; the reveal wipe uses a native range input for full keyboard support.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real fast-neural-style ONNX graph and the displayed image + restyle-amount + downloaded PNG are all derived from the real output tensor. There is NO synthetic pre-degradation; this is a straight photo->painting conversion, so what you see is exactly the model's output.
rubric.notes: Honest by construction. A rendered-output screenshot was reviewed to confirm the style is a genuine repaint (Afremov brushwork), not a filter.

rubric.evidence: All canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-labels (source photo / painting / per-style outputs); every button has an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the loader status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext; the style chips are a labelled role=group with aria-pressed. Light+dark inherit the WCAG-AA design system. Gallery thumbnails are real buttons with aria-pressed + license attribution on selection.
rubric.notes: Consulted modern-web-guidance for accessible live-region status, toggle-button semantics, and exposing canvas content; applied.

rubric.evidence: Mobile+desktop MATRIX measured 0 px horizontal overflow on ALL 5 pages (10/10 measurements) at mobile (360×740, DPR3) AND desktop (1280×800). The two-col layout is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; the param-grid is auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); the style-row flex-wraps; reveal/canvases are inline-size:100%; the wild page's five-panel wall wraps. scripts/responsive-check on the index reported desktop clean · mobile clean.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile download cost (~6.6 MB/style fp32) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense 224²->display resize composite run in the Web Worker; the styled ImageBitmap is transferred, not cloned (invariant 15's dense-output-off-main-thread rule). The main thread only draws bounded fit-canvases + the reveal composite. The fixed 224² network keeps each pass ~0.4 s on single-thread WASM — the fastest of the built image-to-image demos; the UI never blocks and INP stays low.
rubric.notes: The fixed small input is a quality trade-off (fine detail is simplified) but makes the model very fast; the output is resized to the display aspect.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move image inference and resize composite off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks; break up / offload heavy synchronous processing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference AND the 224²->display resize composite run in a dedicated module Web Worker; the styled ImageBitmap is transferred back (zero-copy). The main thread does only bounded canvas draws.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([styledBmp]); UI responsive during stylization; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel with style toggle chips, canvas gallery wall and before/after reveal without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing, logical properties and flexible tracks (fr/minmax) before fixed widths; content-first flexbox with flex-wrap; use min-inline-size:0 so flex children can shrink — fewer media queries, no overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — two-col is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; param-grid is grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); the style-row + five-panel wall flex-wrap; reveal/canvases inline-size:100% block-size:auto; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px horizontal overflow measured on all 5 pages (10/10) at mobile 360×740 DPR3 AND desktop 1280×800

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible toggle button group (style selector) and live region status; expose canvas-rendered content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use a labelled role=group with aria-pressed toggle buttons; announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the style chips are a role=group (aria-labelledby) of aria-pressed buttons; shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvases carry role=img + aria-label; the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; style chips toggle aria-pressed; canvas aria-labels present

openQuestions: The ONNX Model Zoo exports are FIXED at 224² (opset 9), so fine detail is simplified on large photos; a dynamic-size re-export (or tiled inference) would improve output resolution.
The zoo also ships opset-8 variants of the same styles; no quality difference expected, but worth noting if opset-9 ever has a loader issue.

followUpGoals.goal: If a dynamic-input fast-neural-style ONNX (or a clean tiling strategy) is validated, raise the output resolution beyond the fixed 224² — additive, same page.
Add an arbitrary/multi-style transfer demo (e.g. a Magenta arbitrary-style ONNX that takes a content + style image) as a NEW page once its two-input I/O is verified runnable.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for fast-neural-style-transfer (onnxmodelzoo/{candy,mosaic,udnie,pointilism,rain-princess}-9, image-to-image neural style transfer, fp32 ~6.6 MB/style — the classic Johnson-2016 fast-neural-style networks). Built as the wave's SECOND distinct runnable stylizer alongside animegan-cartoonization, because the requested deraining target has no browser-runnable ONNX (recorded status:blocked) and none of the deraining substitutes (JPEG-artifact/retinex/HDR) are runnable either; fast-neural-style is a technically distinct stylization (per-artwork feed-forward perceptual-loss) from the AnimeGAN GAN. RUNNABILITY VERIFIED FIRST in headless Chrome: the real ONNX graphs (fixed 224² NCHW 0-255 I/O) repainted photos, normalized RMSE(out,in) 0.19-0.32, and a rendered-output screenshot confirmed a genuine Afremov 'Rain Princess' repaint (not a colour filter). transformers.js has no style-transfer class, so it runs the ONNX directly via a per-worker onnxruntime-web pin. Full demo driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver: Download ~6.6 MB -> auto-init ready -> real output on the index + ladder pages (basics/practical paint + PNG download; wild five-style gallery wall; multi-model ran BOTH the painting network AND AnimeGANv2 side by side). responsive-check 0 px overflow on all 5 pages (10/10) at desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Fixed 224² keeps it the fastest image-to-image demo (~0.4 s/pass) at the cost of simplified fine detail (disclosed). License Apache-2.0.
